Description: The time and date of the transaction. This excludes the time zone.
The time zone. This is the same as the time zone you specified when you configured the NSS feed.
Seconds (0–59)
Insights Logs: This field is derived from Logged Time

Description: Minutes (0–59)
Insights Logs: This field is derived from Logged Time

Description: Hours (0–23)
Insights Logs: This field is derived from Logged Time

Description: The day of the month (1–31)
Insights Logs: This field is derived from Logged Time

Description: The month of the year
Insights Logs: This field is derived from Logged Time

Insights Logs: This field is derived from Logged Time

Description: The name of the month

Description: The epoch time of the transaction
The client source IP address. For aggregated sessions, this is the client source IP address of the last session in the aggregate.
The client source port. For aggregated sessions, this is the client source port of the last session in the aggregate.
The client destination IP address. For aggregated sessions, this is the client destination IP address of the last session in the aggregate.
The client destination port. For aggregated sessions, this is the client destination port of the last session in the aggregate.
The client destination FDQN (e.g., the HTTP host header)
The tunnel IP address of the client (source). For aggregated sessions, this is the client's tunnel IP address corresponding to the last session in the aggregate.
The name of the location from which the session was initiated
The traffic forwarding method used to send the traffic to the Firewall
Indicates whether the Firewall session is aggregated
The traffic's source country, which is determined by the client IP address location. There is no source country value in logs for aggregated sessions that are allowed.
The category of the threat in the Firewall session by the IPS engine
Example:
Botnet Callback
Denial of Service attack
Malicious Content

Description: The name of the threat detected in the Firewall session by the IPS engine

The score of the threat detected in the Firewall session by the IPS engine. The score is assigned to the threat by Zscaler ThreatLabz and reflected in the Zscaler Threat Library. The score ranges from 0–100, from the least to the greatest threat.

The severity of the threat detected in the Firewall session by the IPS engine. The severity relates directly to the threat score. For example, if the value of %d{threat_score} is between 90 and 100, then the value of %s{threatseverity} is Critical.
The name of the IPS policy that was applied to the Firewall session
Indicates if a custom IPS signature rule was applied. It is a numeric value (1 or 0).
Example:
1 indicates a custom IPS rule
0 indicates a non-custom IPS rule

Description: The server destination port. For aggregated sessions, this is the server destination port of the last session in the aggregate.
The server destination IP address. For aggregated sessions, this is the server destination IP address of the last session in the aggregate.
The server source IP address. For aggregated sessions, this is the server source IP address of the last session in the aggregate.
The server source port. For aggregated sessions, this is the server source port of the last session in the aggregate.
The URL category that corresponds to the server IP address
The average session duration, in milliseconds, if the sessions were aggregated
The session or request duration in seconds
The session or request duration in milliseconds
The number of sessions that were aggregated
Indicates if the Firewall session is stateful
The name of the rule that was applied to the transaction
The action that the service took on the transaction: Allowed or Blocked
Indicates if the destination NAT policy was applied
The name of the destination NAT policy that was applied
Example:
DNAT_Rule_1
Any name under the Rule Name column on the NAT Control page (Policy > Firewall Control > NAT Control Policy)

Description: The record ID
Insights Logs: This field is specific to NSS

Description: The path of the packet capture (PCAP) file that captured the transaction. The PCAP ID has the following format: <Company ID>/<Directory>/<PCAP File Name>. The company ID is the internal ID of an organization and can be found on the Company Profile page. The directory is the log type. To download the PCAP file, go to the Capture column on the Firewall Insights Logs page.
The number of bytes sent from the server to the client
The number of bytes sent from the client to the server
The network application that was accessed
The network service that was used
The type of IP protocol
The abbreviated code of the country of the destination IP address
Indicates if the characters specified in the Feed Escape Character field of the NSS feed configuration page were hex encoded
Insights Logs: This field is specific to NSS

Description: The user's login name in email address format
The department of the user
The hostname of the device
The model of the device
The name of the device
The OS type of the device
Example:
iOS
Android OS
Windows OS
MAC OS
Other OS

Description: The OS version that the device uses
The owner of the device
The app version that the device app uses
The external device ID that associates a user’s device with the mobile device management (MDM) solution
The Z-Tunnel version
Indicates whether the traffic bypassed the Zscaler Client Connector. It is a numeric value (1 or 0).
Example:
1 indicates that the traffic bypassed Zscaler Client Connector
0 indicates that the traffic did not bypass Zscaler Client Connector

Description: The date and time when the traffic bypassed the Zscaler Client Connector
The flow type of the transaction
Example:
Direct
Loopback
VPN
VPN Tunnel
ZIA
ZPA

Description: The name of the data center
The city where the data center is located
The country where the data center is located
The name of the redirect/forwarding policy
Example:
FWD_Rule_1
Any name under the Rule Name column on the Forwarding Control page (Policy > Forwarding Control)

Description: The name of the gateway defined in a forwarding rule
The name of the Zscaler Private Access (ZPA) application segment
